projectDescription: A reusable AI agent skill (structured system prompt) that transforms AI-generated web pages from functional to stunning by injecting design taste, anti-cliché rules, oklch color theory, and a six-step design workflow.
keyFeatures: Anti-cliché rules: explicit blocklist of overused AI design patterns (purple-pink gradients, Inter font, emoji icons, fake testimonials)
Design system declaration step: forces AI to articulate color, typography, spacing, and motion choices before writing code
oklch color theory: perceptually uniform color derivation instead of random hex guessing
Six curated color × font pairings for common use cases (Modern tech, Elegant editorial, Premium brand, etc.)
Placeholder philosophy: professional markers for missing assets instead of poorly drawn SVG fakes
Six-step structured workflow from requirements gathering through verification
Compatible with Claude Code, Cursor, and any AI agent supporting the SKILL.md format
Advanced pattern library with ready-to-use code templates for slides, animations, device frames, and more
publicApiSurface: SKILL.md — main skill definition file (~400 lines) placed in .agents/skills/web-design-engineer/
references/advanced-patterns.md — code template library (~520 lines) for slides, device frames, Tweaks panel, animation timeline, dark mode, oklch color system
Six-step workflow: Understand requirements → Gather design context → Declare design system → Show v0 draft → Full build → Verify
Anti-AI cliché checklist for gradient backgrounds, emoji abuse, left-border accent cards, overused fonts
oklch color derivation system for perceptually uniform palettes

Color × font pairing table: Modern tech (Blue-violet + Space Grotesk), Elegant editorial (Warm brown + Newsreader), Premium brand (Near-black + Sora), Lively consumer (Coral + Plus Jakarta Sans), Minimal professional (Teal-blue + Outfit), Artisan warmth (Caramel + Caveat)
Pre-delivery checklist for verifying output quality
